name: Greek Mythology 2
description: Step up your knowledge with famous punishments, iconic monsters, and the forces of destiny.

questions.question: What happens to anyone who looks directly into the eyes of the Gorgon Medusa?
questions.explain: Medusa has snakes for hair and a gaze so terrifying it petrifies onlookers.

questions.question: What eternal punishment was given to Sisyphus in the underworld?
options: Rolling a boulder up a hill
Standing in water he cannot drink
Spinning on a wheel of fire
questions.explain: Sisyphus must push a massive rock up a hill, only for it to roll back down every time.

questions.question: Which element did the Titan Prometheus steal from the gods to give to humanity?
questions.explain: Prometheus stole fire from Mount Olympus, enabling human civilization and progress.

questions.question: What is the primary role of the Three Fates, also known as the Moirai?
questions.explain: The Three Fates control the metaphorical thread of life for every mortal.

questions.question: Who managed to slay Medusa by using a reflective shield to avoid her direct gaze?
questions.explain: Perseus used a polished bronze shield given by Athena to see Medusa safely.

questions.question: Why did Zeus punish Prometheus by chaining him to a mountain rock?
questions.explain: Zeus was furious that Prometheus gave divine fire to mortals without permission.

questions.question: Which of the Three Fates is responsible for cutting the thread of human life?
questions.explain: Atropos chooses the mechanism of death and cuts the thread with her shears.

questions.question: What creature did Sisyphus famously trick and chain up to temporarily stop human death?
questions.explain: Sisyphus trapped Thanatos, the personification of death, so no humans could die.

questions.question: What monster did Theseus defeat inside the Labyrinth of Crete?
questions.explain: Theseus navigated the maze and killed the half-man, half-bull creature.

questions.question: Which Greek hero was dipped in the River Styx by his mother to become nearly invincible?
questions.explain: Achilles became invulnerable everywhere except for his heel, where his mother held him.
